# Convert hard-coded Saya template strings to i18n keys

## Summary

The Saya tenant theme (components under `components/saya/*`) currently contains a large number of hard-coded, customer-facing strings. These should be converted to use the project's `@nuxtjs/i18n` integration so translations can be added and date formatting can be locale-aware.

This issue collects the audit findings, acceptance criteria, and a step-by-step plan a contributor can follow. The goal is to make the tenant theme translation-ready and to standardize date / locale handling.

## Background / Context

- Repo already includes `@nuxtjs/i18n` and `nuxt.config.ts` is configured with multiple locales.
- Many user-facing strings are hard-coded in templates and script blocks across `components/saya/*` (hero, header, footer, posts, reviews, QA, CTA, upgrade modal, about, etc.).
- Some date formatting is explicitly `en-US` or uses `toLocaleDateString('en-US', ...)`, which must be replaced with a locale-aware approach.

Why this matters:

- Improves localization support for real customers across regions.
- Prevents user-facing copy from being accidentally hard-coded in future UI changes.
- Ensures accessibility strings (ARIA labels) are translatable.

## Audit snapshot (non-exhaustive)

Files and example hard-coded text discovered in the audit (developer should expand as they work):

- `components/saya/SayaHero.vue`
  - `EST.` (establishment prefix)

- `components/saya/SayaHeader.vue`
  - `Menu`, `Locations`, `Reservations`, `Experiences`, `Contact`, `Order Now`
  - ARIA: `Open navigation`, `Close navigation`

- `components/saya/SayaFooter.vue`
  - `We could not load locations right now. Please try again in a moment.`
  - `Visit page →`
  - Headings: `Experience`, `Discover`, `Connect`
  - Links/labels: `Menu`, `Experiences`, `Gallery`, `Our Story`, `Reviews`, `Latest Updates`, `Q&A`, `All Locations`, `Contact Us`, `Order online`, `Privacy`, `Terms`, `Powered by krabiclaw.com, ...`

- `components/saya/SayaPosts.vue`
  - `Latest Updates`, `News & Events`, `No preview available`, badges `Event`/`Offer`/`Update`, `Restaurant update`, `Business Update`, `Event Details:`, `Special Offer:`, `Read Full Story`, `View All Updates`

- `components/saya/SayaReviews.vue`
  - `Guest Experience`, `What Our Guests Say`, `Restaurant Response:`, `No reviews yet.`, `Be the first to share your experience with us.`, `View All Reviews`, `Anonymous`

- `components/saya/SayaSubNav.vue`
  - Tab labels: `Overview`, `Menu`, `Reviews`, `Photos`, `Q&A`, `Visit`

- `components/saya/SayaCTA.vue`
  - `Order Now`, `Reserve` (reserveCta fallback)

- `components/saya/SayaUpgradeModal.vue`
  - Feature titles/descriptions in `featureMap` and `featureBullets` (e.g. `Ask a question`, `Custom domain`, `Add a second location`, `Connect Google Business`, `Google Business sync`)

- `components/saya/SayaAbout.vue`
  - `Authentic Experience` placeholder, `Our Story`, default description, quote `"Happy Guest, Happy Restaurant"`, CTA `Our Full Story`

- `components/saya/SayaQA.vue`
  - `Questions & Answers`, `Common Inquiries`, `Google Guest`, `Response from Restaurant`, `No answer yet.`, `Got a question? Ask us anything about our menu or service.`, `View All Q&A`

Other common patterns:

- Inline UI characters such as `→`, `★`, `☆`, ellipses are used; keep or extract to i18n if layout/spacing differs by locale.
- Date formatting frequently uses an `en-US` locale — update to use `i18n.locale.value`.
- Some copy comes from `verticalCopy` or `copy` utilities; verify these values are already localized or move them to i18n.

## Acceptance criteria

1. A `locales/en.json` is added (or expanded) and contains a comprehensive `saya` key with English defaults for all changed strings.
2. All hard-coded user-facing strings inside `components/saya/*` are replaced with i18n lookups (`{{ $t('...') }}` in templates or `const { t } = useI18n()` in scripts).
3. Date formatting uses the active locale (e.g., `new Intl.DateTimeFormat(i18n.locale.value, opts)`), not a fixed `'en-US'` string.
4. ARIA labels and important accessibility strings are localized.
5. Linting and type checks pass: `yarn lint` and `yarn typecheck`.
6. PR includes before/after screenshots or a short QA checklist demonstrating strings changed.

## Detailed tasks (suggested order)

1. Create `locales/en.json` skeleton with a top-level `saya` object and keys for header/footer/hero/posts/reviews/qa/cta/upgrade/about. (See sample below.)
2. Replace header strings:
   - Use `$t('saya.header.menu')`, etc., including ARIA strings for mobile menu toggle.
3. Replace footer strings:
   - Section headings, nav links, error messages, legal text, and `Powered by` copy.
4. Replace hero and CTA strings.
5. Replace posts/reviews/qa component strings, badges, empty-state text, and alt text fallbacks.
6. Update `featureMap` and `featureBullets` in `SayaUpgradeModal.vue` to be keyed entries (e.g., `saya.upgrade.features.connect-gmb.title`).
7. Replace in-script fallbacks (e.g., `primaryCtaLabel` fallback `'Order Now'`) to use `t('...')` by default.
8. Update date formatting in `SayaPosts.vue`, `SayaReviews.vue`, `SayaQA.vue` to use the i18n locale (get `const { locale } = useI18n()` and pass `locale.value` to `Intl.DateTimeFormat`).
9. Run `yarn lint && yarn typecheck` and fix any issues.
10. Add a short PR checklist in the PR description: list of files changed, testing steps, screenshots.

## Sample `locales/en.json` skeleton (contributor should expand as they work)

```json
{
  "saya": {
    "hero": {
      "established_prefix": "EST."
    },
    "header": {
      "menu": "Menu",
      "locations": "Locations",
      "reservations": "Reservations",
      "experiences": "Experiences",
      "contact": "Contact",
      "order_now": "Order Now",
      "open_navigation": "Open navigation",
      "close_navigation": "Close navigation"
    },
    "footer": {
      "locations_error": "We could not load locations right now. Please try again in a moment.",
      "visit_page": "Visit page →",
      "heading_experience": "Experience",
      "heading_discover": "Discover",
      "heading_connect": "Connect",
      "order_online": "Order online",
      "powered_by": "Powered by krabiclaw.com"
    },
    "posts": {
      "title": "Latest Updates",
      "subtitle": "News & Events",
      "no_preview": "No preview available",
      "badge": { "event": "Event", "offer": "Offer", "update": "Update" },
      "image_alt": "Restaurant update",
      "event_details_label": "Event Details:",
      "special_offer_label": "Special Offer:",
      "read_full_story": "Read Full Story",
      "view_all": "View All Updates"
    },
    "reviews": {
      "title": "Guest Experience",
      "subtitle": "What Our Guests Say",
      "response_label": "Restaurant Response:",
      "empty_title": "No reviews yet.",
      "empty_desc": "Be the first to share your experience with us.",
      "view_all": "View All Reviews",
      "anonymous": "Anonymous"
    },
    "subnav": {
      "overview": "Overview",
      "menu": "Menu",
      "reviews": "Reviews",
      "photos": "Photos",
      "qa": "Q&A",
      "visit": "Visit"
    },
    "cta": {
      "order_now": "Order Now",
      "reserve": "Reserve"
    },
    "upgrade": {
      "features": {
        "connect-gmb": {
          "title": "Connect Google Business",
          "description": "Sync hours, reviews, photos, and posts automatically from your Google Business Profile."
        }
      }
    },
    "about": {
      "our_story": "Our Story",
      "placeholder": "Authentic Experience",
      "quote": "Happy Guest, Happy Restaurant",
      "cta": "Our Full Story"
    },
    "qa": {
      "title": "Questions & Answers",
      "subtitle": "Common Inquiries",
      "google_guest": "Google Guest",
      "response_from_restaurant": "Response from Restaurant",
      "no_answer": "No answer yet.",
      "empty_prompt": "Got a question? Ask us anything about our menu or service.",
      "view_all": "View All Q&A"
    }
  },
  "legal": {
    "privacy": "Privacy",
    "terms": "Terms"
  }
}
```

## How to test locally

1. Create a branch for the work (example):

```bash
git checkout -b i18n/saya-header-footer
```

2. Implement changes and add `locales/en.json` or extend existing file.
3. Run the dev server and verify strings on sample tenant pages:

```bash
yarn install
yarn dev
```

4. Run checks:

```bash
yarn lint
yarn typecheck
```

5. Include before/after screenshots in the PR description.

## PR checklist for contributors

- [ ] Add/extend `locales/en.json` with all keys used in changes.
- [ ] Replaced template literals with `$t()` and replaced programmatic strings with `t()`.
- [ ] Updated date formatting to use `i18n.locale.value`.
- [ ] Verified ARIA/accessibility strings are localized.
- [ ] `yarn lint` and `yarn typecheck` pass.
- [ ] Added screenshots and QA notes to PR description.

## Labels

- `good first issue`, `i18n`, `frontend`, `help wanted`

## Estimated effort

- Small incremental PRs (header/footer/hero): 2–4 hours.
- Full pass across all Saya components and date formatting: 6–12 hours depending on PR scope.

---

If you want, I can follow up and:

- Create a branch and implement the header/footer/hero replacements as a starter PR.
- Or split this issue into smaller issues per component area (`i18n/saya-header`, `i18n/saya-footer`, etc.) so contributors can pick.

Please let me know how you'd like to split the work and whether I should open the starter PR.
